mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward returns show no statistical significance when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating household income growth, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment rises above 6%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 20-day returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of stable inflation reflects healthy capacity utilization without price pressures, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annually, or 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of low-inflation expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the opportunity cost of holding equities relative to risk-free assets, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive FEDFUNDS changes correlate with positive 5-day returns in > 55% of observations, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll additions exceeding 200,000 signal robust economic momentum that outweighs wage inflation concerns, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers negative 20-day returns in > 60% of months where payroll growth exceeds the 200,000 threshold, or the signal fails during labor market tightness.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP growth and falling real personal consumption expenditures indicates corporate earnings are driven by non-consumer sectors, producing negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence fails to predict negative 60-day returns, or the interaction effect vanishes when consumer spending remains above trend.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp declines in industrial production during monetary tightening cycles reflect demand destruction rather than supply shocks, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods produce positive 5-day returns in > 50% of cases, or the mechanism loses predictive power when rate hikes are priced in.
